title: DLRS Derived Asset Descriptor
description: Provenance and lineage metadata for an artefact produced by a DLRS build pipeline (ASR transcript, cleaned text, embedding index, moderation report, ...). Every concrete pipeline implementation under pipelines/ MUST emit one descriptor next to its primary output so that downstream consumers can re-derive, verify, or audit the result. v0.5 introduces the schema; the actual emitter contracts land alongside each pipeline body (issues #31-#34).

schema_version.description: Version of this schema. Bump on any breaking change. Pipelines SHOULD pin to a specific schema_version so downstream verifiers can detect drift.

derived_id.description: Stable identifier for this derived artefact within the owning record. ULID or UUID4. Once written it MUST NOT change; re-running the pipeline produces a new derived_id even if the inputs are identical, so historical descriptors stay attributable.

record_id.description: Owning DLRS record id. MUST match the manifest.record_id of the directory under which this descriptor lives. Cross-record derivations are out of scope; emit one descriptor per record.

pipeline.description: Pipeline that produced this artefact. MUST equal the SPEC.name of a registered pipeline under pipelines/.

pipeline_version.description: Implementation version of the pipeline (NOT the model version, see model.* below). SemVer or commit SHA fragment. Used together with inputs_hash to decide whether a re-run is necessary.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the pipeline finished writing the output. Pipelines SHOULD use the time of write, not the time the run started.

actor_role.description: Who triggered the pipeline run. Mirrors the audit-event actor_role enum so emit_audit_event.py can quote it directly in a build_started event.

inputs.description: Provenance of the inputs that fed the pipeline. The combination of source_pointers + inputs_hash is what makes a re-run reproducible.

source_pointers.description: Relative paths (from the record root) to every pointer.json or raw artefact consumed. Pointer files are preferred over raw paths so the hash check survives a storage migration.

inputs_hash.description: sha256 of the canonical concatenation of input file content hashes (one per source_pointer, in declared order). Pipelines MUST refuse to re-emit an identical descriptor if inputs_hash and pipeline_version both match.

preprocessing.description: Free-form structured record of any pre-pipeline transforms (resampling, transcoding, NFKC, ...). Schema-validated content SHOULD live in named keys.

model.description: Optional ML model identification. REQUIRED when the pipeline is asr or vectorization; otherwise MAY be omitted.

id.description: Canonical model identifier (HF model id, local model name, ...).

version.description: Specific revision: HF revision SHA, model release tag, or pipeline-supplied version string.

source.description: Where the model came from. v0.5 is offline-first so this is normally a local path or a cached HF revision; hosted-API usage is forbidden in v0.5.

online_api_used.description: MUST be false in v0.5 (offline-first invariant enforced by tools/validate_pipelines.py). Reserved for v0.6 online-enhanced pipelines.

parameters.description: Pipeline-specific kwargs serialised verbatim (chunk size, language hint, embedding dim, lexicon path, ...). Anything that influences the output MUST be recorded here so re-runs are reproducible.

output.description: Description of the canonical output artefact.

path.description: Relative path from the record root, MUST start with derived/<pipeline>/.

outputs_hash.description: sha256 of the produced file's bytes (or, for multi-file outputs like a Qdrant collection, of the canonical manifest of those files).

byte_size.description: Optional convenience field; MUST equal the output file size in bytes when set.

audit_event_ref.description: Optional reference to a build_started / build_completed audit event (file path + line number, e.g. 'audit/events.jsonl#L12'). Lets reviewers jump from a derived asset to the audit log entry that authorised it.

moderation_outcome.description: For pipelines that produce moderation results, one of 'pass', 'flag', 'block'. Other pipelines MAY set this if their output triggers downstream policy decisions.

metadata.description: Free-form additive metadata. Schema-validated content SHOULD live under one of the named top-level fields above instead.
